message:
feat(runtime): add finalizer lifecycle tracking and cleanup-task admission bypass

Runtime state finalizer tracking (state.rs):
- Add FinalizerHistoryEvent enum (Registered, Ran, RegionClosed) and
  append-only finalizer_history vec for post-run oracle hydration.
- Track pending finalizer IDs per region via pending_finalizer_ids
  HashMap, maintaining a LIFO stack parallel to the region's finalizer
  barrier stack.
- Track async finalizer tasks via async_finalizer_tasks HashMap so
  task_completed can record the finalizer run event.
- New methods: allocate_finalizer_id, record_finalizer_registration,
  record_finalizer_run, record_finalizer_close, pop_tracked_finalizer,
  run_sync_finalizers_tracked.
- register_sync_finalizer and register_async_finalizer now allocate
  tracked IDs and record registration events.
- spawn_finalizer_task now returns Result with the future on failure
  (instead of Option), and the caller re-enqueues the async barrier
  with its tracked ID preserved so the region cannot close with
  cleanup silently dropped.
- create_task_infrastructure gains a cleanup_task bool parameter so
  finalizer-spawned tasks can bypass the region's max_tasks limit
  during the Finalizing phase.
- drain_ready_async_finalizers and advance_region_lifecycle use tracked
  variants and record close events.
- Added tests: sync finalizer history lifecycle, async finalizer
  history lifecycle, async cleanup task scheduled even with zero task
  limit.

Region record cleanup task admission (region.rs):
- Extract add_task_internal with bypass_task_limit_in_finalizing flag.
- add_task (existing) always enforces the limit.
- add_cleanup_task (new) bypasses the limit when the region is in
  Finalizing state, ensuring required cleanup work runs even if the
  region was configured with max_tasks=0.
- Added test: cleanup_task_bypasses_task_limit_when_finalizing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
